alt_text: A calligram of the Arabic definite article الـ (al-, formed by the letters Alif and Lam) rendered in large yellow strokes. Annotated in small blue text: the word عقل (Intellect) is placed at the top against the Alif (ا), and the word نفس (Self) is placed inside the curve of the Lam (ل). The illustration teaches the doctrine that the two letters forming the Arabic definite article symbolically represent the two primary spiritual principles — Intellect (mapped to Alif, the upright letter) and Self (mapped to Lam, the descending letter). Matches the topic title: علامت معرفۃ: الف اور لام (The Sign of Definition: Alif and Lam).
notes: No Quran content. Diagram is a visual play on the letters of the definite article that introduces this section's argument.
